decisions.reasoning: MSFT is down ~12.7% on the position and the stock has fallen >20% in June 2026, its worst month since 2000, driven by AI capex/margin-compression fears tied to the ~$190B 2026 capex plan and the Chevron power deal, a critical Nature article (Jun 24) questioning the Majorana quantum roadmap, Xbox hardware/gaming weakness (~1,000 layoffs), and a new executive order requiring AI model review. Fundamentals remain strong (FY26 Q3 revenue +18% to $82.9B, EPS +21%, AI ARR +123% YoY to $37B) and consensus price targets ($589-592) imply large upside, so this looks like an overdone selloff rather than a broken thesis. No fresh today-dated catalyst justifies adding or trimming, and SMA signal is flat, so holding the existing 3 shares.

sources.headline: Microsoft Sees Major Stock Decline Amid AI Concerns
Microsoft Corp Stock (MSFT) Moved Down by 3.80% on Jun 25

decisions.invalidation: If FY26 Q4 guidance shows AI capex translating into accelerating Azure/AI revenue without further FCF margin erosion, the selloff thesis weakens and MSFT becomes a candidate to add; if capex keeps rising while AI ARR growth decelerates, or further credible quantum-roadmap retractions emerge, exit the position.

decisions.reasoning: NVDA is down ~9% on the position. Q1 FY27 data center revenue still grew 92% YoY to $75.25B and the board added an $80B buyback (May 18), but bearish signals have accumulated: China data-center revenue is effectively zero per Jensen Huang's May admission that NVIDIA has 'largely conceded' China to Huawei, the Commerce Department closed an offshore-subsidiary loophole in early June, and cloud B200 rental rates fell ~31% in three weeks (May 30-Jun 21), a real-time signal of cooling GPU demand/oversupply. These are credible, multi-sourced risks rather than single-headline noise, but none is a fresh today-dated catalyst and the SMA signal is flat, so this isn't a high-conviction sell point yet - watching closely rather than trimming into an already realized ~9% drawdown without a clearer trigger.

sources.headline: Why Is NVIDIA Stock Falling? China, AI Demand & Valuation
Nvidia (NVDA) Stock Trades Down, Here Is Why
Q1 FY2027 earnings: Data Center revenue +92% YoY, $80B buyback authorized

decisions.invalidation: A further material drop in cloud GPU rental pricing, confirmation of decelerating hyperscaler capex commitments, or additional China export-control tightening would tip this toward a trim; a stabilization/rebound in GPU rental pricing or a China policy reversal would support holding or adding.

decisions.reasoning: QQQ position is roughly flat (-1.7%) and the SMA signal is flat; broad tech is mixed today (Nasdaq +0.29% intraday per TheStreet, Jun 30) after Monday's Dow record close, with no fresh catalyst strong enough to change broad index exposure. Continuing to hold as a core market-beta sleeve alongside SPY.

sources.headline: Stock Market Today (June 30, 2026): Dow edges lower after record close

decisions.invalidation: A sharp deterioration in mega-cap tech earnings trajectory or a hawkish Fed surprise that breaks the broad uptrend would prompt trimming broad index exposure.

decisions.reasoning: SPY position is modestly profitable (+1.4%) and SMA signal is flat. Market context is mixed-to-firm (S&P 500 +0.03% intraday, Dow just closed above 52,000 for the first time Monday) but geopolitical risk is rising again - Iran accused the US of violating the June 17 ceasefire on Jun 30, reviving Strait of Hormuz escalation concerns that pushed oil higher earlier in June. Given the two-sided risk (record highs vs. fresh Middle East tension), holding the existing core index exposure rather than adding or trimming is the prudent call.

sources.headline: Stock Market Today (June 30, 2026): Dow edges lower after record close
Iran Accuses US of Ceasefire Violation

decisions.invalidation: A confirmed major escalation (e.g., Strait of Hormuz closure or direct US-Iran military exchange) that spikes oil and risk-off flows broadly would warrant trimming broad equity beta; a durable ceasefire resolution would support holding/adding.

decisions.reasoning: Considered XLE as a new candidate given the Strait of Hormuz crisis (Brent ~$105/bbl, XLE +38.2% YTD), but the rally has already happened and the situation is whipsawing - a June 17 ceasefire and US officials saying Hormuz traffic is 'back to normal' were followed by today's (Jun 30) mutual ceasefire-violation accusations. Chasing an already-extended, binary geopolitical trade this late, with gross long exposure already at ~91% of equity (cap 95%), fails the FOMO/gap-up discipline and leaves almost no room under the exposure cap anyway. Passing for now.

sources.headline: Oil prices continue slide amid hopes for peace, opening of Strait of Hormuz
Iran Accuses US of Ceasefire Violation; IT and Auto Stocks Drag

decisions.invalidation: A clean, durable ceasefire with falling oil prices would remove the catalyst entirely (skip permanently); a confirmed major escalation/closure of the Strait would make XLE/energy a higher-conviction add, but only with fresh, dated confirmation, not a headline alone.

summary: First journal entry (no prior history): held all four existing positions (MSFT, NVDA, QQQ, SPY) - both single names face credible but already-known bearish narratives (MSFT AI-capex/margin concerns, NVDA China/GPU-pricing softness) without a fresh dated catalyst to act on; skipped chasing XLE into an already-extended, whipsawing Iran/oil story. No orders placed.
open_theses: MSFT: watching FY26 Q4 guidance for AI capex-to-revenue conversion and further quantum-roadmap credibility news; large valuation gap to analyst targets ($589-592) makes this a potential add on stabilization.
NVDA: watching cloud GPU rental pricing trend (B200 rates down ~31% in 3 weeks) and any further China export-control tightening as leading indicators of demand softness.
Geopolitical: watching Iran-US ceasefire status and Strait of Hormuz traffic for risk-on/risk-off implications across SPY/QQQ and as a potential trigger to revisit XLE.
